clinical_note.history_of_present_illness: Emily Jackson is a 40-year-old female presenting with persistent dryness and irritation in her right eye over the past three months. Symptoms have not improved with over-the-counter artificial tears. Reports intermittent mild blurred vision, no pain but some discomfort, occasional morning stinging, no itching, no significant light sensitivity, and a gritty sensation. No history of injury, surgery, or infection to the eyes. No symptoms in the left eye.

previous_history.social_history: Non-smoker, occasional alcohol use, lives with husband and kids

clinical_note.physical_exam: Right eye examination reveals conjunctival injection and reduced tear meniscus height. Fluorescein staining shows mild punctate epithelial erosions in the right eye. Tear break-up time is less than 5 seconds on the right eye. Left eye exam normal.

assessment.summary: Dry eye syndrome of the right lacrimal gland.

clinical_note.plan: Prescribe cyclosporine ophthalmic emulsion 0.05% for the right eye, one drop twice daily. Recommend warm compresses. Encourage frequent use of artificial tears. Follow up in six weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.
